_README: Seed data and exact entity shapes for DepFlow. Port the algorithm in _ALGORITHM directly. _EXPECTED_LAYERS lets you unit-test computeLayers().

issue.title: string
issue.deps: array of issue ids this issue depends on (global, cross-wave allowed)
issue.parentId: string|null (set on children of an epic)
issue.children: array of {id,title} OR model as issues with parentId

_ALGORITHM.computeLayers: For a given wave W: take issues where issue.wave==W into set S. depth(id) = (deps in S).length ? 1 + max(depth(d) for d in deps that are in S) : 0. Dependencies NOT in S are ignored for layer math. Group ids by depth. Layer 0 = 'start here'. Memoize. Guard against cycles.
_ALGORITHM.deriveState: if issue.done -> 'done'. else if every dep (across ALL waves) is done -> 'active'. else -> 'blocked'.
_ALGORITHM.issueDetail_dependencies: When showing an issue's detail, list ALL deps regardless of wave. Tag each with its wave; if dep.done show a check. Cross-wave deps are shown but never block the wave-filtered Ordine view.
_ALGORITHM.reverseEdges_unblocks: issues B where B.deps includes thisId.

projects.name: Aplicație Turism
projects.description: Expense tracker + bilete + auth pentru călătorii

issues.title: Setări → Management utilizatori
issues.desc: Tichetul părinte e gata doar când toate sub-tichetele sunt gata.

_EXPECTED_LAYERS._note: Output of computeLayers per wave. Keys are layer depth -> issue ids. Use as unit-test fixtures.

_EXPECTED_CROSSWAVE_EXAMPLE.note: In Val 2 view, TUR-07 sits at layer 1 (after TUR-API). Its dep TUR-06 lives in Val 1 and is ignored for Val-2 layer math, but appears in TUR-07's detail sheet tagged 'Val 1' so the user still sees it.
